{{Quote|For a Jedi to interfere with a culture on their homeworld would have been to pass judgment on them on behalf of the Republic.|Master Marspa|<ref name="POTJ" />}}
'''Marspa''' was a [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] who served the [[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]] and the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] sometime after the [[Ruusan Reformations/Legends|Ruusan Reformations]] that served as the catalyst for the [[Golden Age of the Old Republic]].

==Biography==
A [[Force-sensitive/Legends|Force-sensitive]] [[Gender/Legends|male]], Marspa was [[Jedi training/Legends|educated]] in the ways of [[The Force/Legends|the Force]] by the [[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]], eventually attaining the rank of [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]]. During his years of service to the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]], Master Marspa selected the [[Jedi Initiate/Legends|Initiate]] [[Imina]] as his [[Padawan/Legends|Padawan]]. Traveling [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]] together, Master Marspa tried to instill the meaning of the [[Jedi Code/Legends|Jedi Code]] on Padawan Imina over the years. While traveling, Padawan Imina grew confused as to why the Jedi could intervene to prevent domestic violence on [[Ord Mantell/Legends|Ord Mantell]] but not slavery on [[Nal Hutta/Legends|Nal Hutta]].<ref name="POTJ" />

Master Marspa explained that because Ord Mantell was a member of the [[Galactic Senate/Legends|Galactic Senate]], the Order could intervene and assist the government. Because Nal Hutta remained outside the galaxy-spanning government, for the Jedi to act would be to impose the laws of the Republic on non-member states and their citizens.<ref name="POTJ" />
